title: ForgeSubscriptionError
description: Emitted by deer-flow's ForgeBusConsumer when an inbound bead-lifecycle event cannot be processed. Surfaces parse failures, missing-correlation errors, and handler exceptions so operators (pulse dashboard, schema-coverage CI) can observe Forge handshake health. Backfilled 2026-05-17 — the emitter at deer-flow/backend/packages/harness/deerflow/bus.py:744 pre-existed without a schema, in violation of CLAUDE.md schema-first rule. Tracked: nervous-bus-cmug, deer-flow-mcyv.

event_channel.description: The bus channel of the inbound event that failed processing (e.g. 'bus.bead.lifecycle.v1', 'bus.bead.bench_completed.v1'). Provides the operator a direct pointer to the producer surface that drifted.

bead_id.description: Bead identifier from the inbound event payload, if present. Null when the event couldn't be parsed far enough to extract a bead_id, OR when the inbound channel doesn't carry one.

error_type.description: Short machine-readable error class. Known values: 'parse_error' (envelope malformed), 'session_not_found' (no active Forge session for bead_id), 'handler_exception' (handler raised), plus any Python exception class name when the cause is a raised exception.

message.description: Human-readable error detail, truncated to 500 chars by the emitter. Includes traceback context for handler exceptions.
